apps.name: The Weather Channel
apps.summary: FTC 2019 complaint: MAID + precise GPS sold to hedge funds and advertisers via Cuebiq without adequate user disclosure.

apps.name: AccuWeather
apps.summary: Caught in 2017 transmitting MAID + GPS to Reveal Mobile even when location permission was denied — used WiFi network names as fallback.

apps.name: GasBuddy
apps.summary: NYT investigation found GasBuddy pinging location over 14,000 times in 24 hours, transmitting MAID+GPS to multiple brokers.

apps.name: Muslim Pro
apps.summary: Sold MAID-linked GPS data to X-Mode, which resold to US military contractors (JSOC, NGA). App had 98M downloads at time of exposure.

apps.name: Life360
apps.summary: Sold continuous MAID+GPS streams of 31M users including children to multiple data brokers. Marketed as a family safety app.

apps.name: Strava
apps.summary: Global heatmap exposed patrol routes and bases of intelligence/military personnel. Even 'private' activities can expose sensitive location patterns via MAID profile.

apps.name: Grindr
apps.summary: MAID linked to precise location and HIV status sold to advertising partners. Norwegian DPA fined €6.5M. Data de-anonymized to identify military/government personnel.

apps.name: TikTok
apps.summary: Collects MAID, device fingerprint (IMEI, MAC, SIM serial), clipboard content, and location. ByteDance engineers confirmed access to US user data from China.

apps.name: Facebook
apps.summary: Facebook SDK embedded in 8M+ apps transmits MAID from third-party apps back to Meta. Location data collected even when user is not using Facebook.

apps.name: Candy Crush Saga
apps.summary: Embeds multiple advertising SDKs that collect MAID and pass it through real-time bidding (RTB) auctions, exposing it to hundreds of ad buyers.

apps.name: Truecaller
apps.summary: Uploads full contact list and device identifiers including MAID without granular consent. Investigated by multiple EU DPAs.

apps.name: Meitu
apps.summary: Requests IMEI, MAC address, MAID, and cell tower data. Transmits to servers in China. Security researchers flagged extensive device fingerprinting beyond stated functionality.

apps.name: Flightradar24
apps.summary: Usage of this app near military airbases or by aviation-adjacent personnel creates trackable MAID+GPS history shared with third-party analytics.

apps.name: Weather Underground
apps.summary: Owned by The Weather Company (IBM). Shares MAID+GPS under same data-sharing framework as The Weather Channel.

apps.name: Foursquare / Swarm
brokers: Foursquare (self — resells to hedge funds, advertisers)
apps.summary: Foursquare itself is a data broker. Its Pilgrim SDK harvests MAID+GPS from thousands of apps. Data sold to financial firms and advertisers for foot-traffic intelligence.
